Explain the difference between RAG and fine-tuning in LLMs.
RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) retrieves external documents at inference time to ground responses in up-to-date facts. Fine-tuning updates the model weights using domain-specific training data, making it better at specific tasks but without real-time knowledge access.
Which approach is better for a production Q&A system?
For a Q&A system that needs up-to-date information, RAG is usually preferred since you can update the document store without retraining. Fine-tuning is better when you need specific linguistic or behavioral patterns baked into the model.
